entry.title: Re: Is it possible to use SVT classic along with the rest...
entry.content:
Hello Paulo

the answer depends on the "risks"/"challenges" you will accept and your current landscape and current use of software.

In a nutshell: 

Topic 1: you can use EHS Classic and Product Compliance in parallel if you accept double maintenance and "double" integration to logistic processes. But you need to pay attention with "how you use Product Compliance"

In the current Product Compiance software (on premise version): you have suboptions to "activate" the software (Product Compliance). But if you e.g. activate the "DG" management: You should not use DG in EHS Classic any more. Here you must/should be strict.

So the "double integration" (regarding DG) means: it is not foreseen to use DG EHS Classic and DG Product Compliance (according to my knowledge) in parallel. But you can still use e.g. EHS Classic for e.g. SVT, Report Shipment and other stuff and use DG in Product Compliance

Topic 2: The use of the "3E Adapter" might help you to avoid partially double maintenance. BUT you must have used in EHS Classic 3E aspects. Mainly: Property tree, phrases. WWI Layout and rulesets are not a "must" but the 3E adapter (according to best knowledge) assumes that you use the SAP/3E Standard property tree and 3E phrases only (and SAP / 3E Standard data model).

Topic 3: to use "Product Compliance": you must "forget" old rules from EHS classic but must accept "new" rules from "Product Compliance"

So e.g. the "dangerous goods profile" (and many other stuff) on material level is of interest for EHS Classic BUT not for Product Compliance. Here: the material must be "relevant" for Product Compliance. AND THIS YOU CAN ONLY ACHIEVE if you do not use MM01 but you must use the corresponding Fiori app. There is no other option (from User perspective)

So the use of "Product Compliance" is a challenge for "Business" and "IT Organisation" (and i skip here that the Product Compliance does have a completey different Access Concept in comparison to EHS Classic)

Wish you the best 

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Brownfield Conversion from EHS Product Safety (ECC) t...
entry.content:
Hello

the issue is the term "- As far as I understood, in a brownfield conversion the master data will be copied from the ECC system to the S/4 System. If there are master data which cannot be copied and has to be migrated manually, then I would really appreciate if you could list them here"

more specific: Copy the master data

This the "trouble"; and it is a good idea to do data cleansing befor: therefore: do a copy of master data only if it is "active" and needed. This reduces the amount of data a lot (KEEP IN MIND: even if you just "copy" the data: somebody must "validate" that the copy is "ok"

And you have a huge liste of versions how to do the "copy". Some of them is supported by SAP; some other you must do manually

And in most of the projects i know.. a wave approach is used this makes the "migration part" more complex

IN a nutshell. If you come from EHS classic and you go on with EHS Classic.. this is possible, But sometimes other (so called workstreams) change their set up as e.g. SAP SD, PP etc. and as a fact of that you must do afterwork in EHS classic

Wish You the best

C.B.

entry.title: Addressing Inventory Mismatch in Warehousing using SAP S/4HANA EWM: A Case Study from Germany
entry.content:
Inventory mismatch is one of the most persistent and impactful challenges faced by businesses across industries, ranging from e-commerce to manufacturing. It affects not only sales order fulfillment but also leads to significant financial losses when discrepancies in inventory valuation arise. From my past projects, I recall one such case involving a manufacturing giant in Germany ,which highlights the importance of effective warehouse management systems and ERP integration.

The Problem: Missing Stock During Transportation. The client, a leading manufacturer, operates multiple small warehouses alongside a central production plant. Despite using SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) to manage stock at the warehouse level, discrepancies frequently arose. Stock often went missing during the short transport journey between the production plant and the warehouse.

A site analysis revealed the root of the issue:

1. Absence of ERP at the Production Plant:

The production plant lacked an appropriate ERP system like SAP PP (Production Planning) to manage stock effectively. Instead, it relied on outdated, paper-based counting systems, which led to errors, delays, and a lack of transparency.

2. Manual Processes at the Warehouse Level:

Even at the warehouse level, manual practices such as paper-based inventory counting and bin sorting were prevalent. These processes not only contributed to inventory mismatches but also significantly hampered operational efficiency.

The Impact of Inventory Mismatch: The financial repercussions for the company were considerable. Missing stock meant not only a direct loss in inventory value but also disruptions in production and delayed order fulfillment. These inefficiencies cascaded across the supply chain, impacting customer satisfaction and the company’s bottom line.

The Solution: Implementing Robust SAP EWM Features.

To address these challenges, we implemented a series of measures leveraging SAP EWM’s capabilities:

1. SAP EWM Deconsolidation:

Deconsolidation is the process of breaking down large incoming shipments into smaller, more manageable units for efficient put-away and retrieval. By implementing SAP EWM's deconsolidation functionality, we ensured that products were properly sorted and organized before being stored in the warehouse. This streamlined the process and reduced errors during inventory handling.

2. SAP EWM Physical Inventory Counting:

We introduced SAP EWM’s physical inventory counting features to replace manual counting methods. This enabled automated and accurate inventory checks, reducing discrepancies and enhancing overall transparency.

3. SAP PP-EWM Integration at the Production Plant Level:

To address the lack of ERP at the production plant, we integrated SAP PP with SAP EWM. This integration provided real-time visibility into inventory movement from the production plant to the warehouse. It also automated key processes, ensuring seamless tracking and synchronization of stock levels.

Results Achieved:

The implementation of these solutions delivered several tangible benefits to the client:

Improved Transparency:

Real-time data flow between the production plant and the warehouse eliminated blind spots in inventory tracking.

Reduced Financial Losses:

By minimizing inventory mismatches, the company significantly reduced the financial impact of missing stock.

Enhanced Efficiency:

Automation replaced manual processes, speeding up operations and allowing employees to focus on higher-value tasks.

Better Order Fulfillment:

With accurate inventory data, the company could fulfill sales orders more reliably, improving customer satisfaction.

Key Takeaways:

This case underscores the critical importance of integrating robust ERP and warehouse management systems in addressing inventory mismatches. Here are some key lessons learned:

1. ERP Integration is Essential:

A lack of ERP tools at the production plant was the primary cause of the issue. Integrating SAP PP with SAP EWM provided the necessary transparency and control.

2. Eliminate Manual Processes:

Paper-based systems are prone to errors and inefficiencies. Transitioning to automated processes not only reduces discrepancies but also enhances operational efficiency.

3. Leverage Advanced Features of SAP EWM:

Functions like deconsolidation and physical inventory counting are powerful tools for optimizing warehouse operations. Companies should utilize these features to address specific challenges.

4. Adopt a Holistic Approach:

Warehouse management doesn’t exist in isolation. Issues at the production plant level can ripple through the supply chain. A holistic approach, addressing both production and warehousing, is crucial.

Conclusion: 

Inventory mismatch is a common yet solvable issue that affects businesses across industries. By adopting the right technologies and integrating processes, companies can mitigate losses, improve efficiency, and enhance customer satisfaction. The German manufacturing giant’s case serves as a testament to the transformative impact of leveraging SAP EWM and ERP tools effectively. For organizations looking to streamline their operations, this approach provides a blueprint for success.#SAPEWM SAP Supply Chain Management  SAP Extended Warehouse Management SAP ERP, add-on for embedded production planning and detailed scheduling

entry.title: Re: Account determination for KORS
Control Asset Master Change Screen (Tabs / Fields) For Different Groups of Users
entry.content:
Business Case:

For Asset Master Data Change process (AS02), there are three sets / groups of users, and each one should have access to certain tabs and the fields under such tabs. The tab, and fields therein, which are visible/accessible to one group of users, shall be invisible for other group of users. Objective is to provide specific data access for each user group so they won’t be able to access/change Asset Master data which is out of their authorization domain.

In  nutshell, the Asset Master screen layout will vary depending on the group a particular user belongs / assigned to.

Scenario For 1st Group of Users:

This group users shall be able to edit only one field “Personnel Number” under the tab “Time-dependent”, rest all fields should be in read-only mode.

Following users to be assigned to 1st group:

FI_TEST_1

FI_TEST_3

Scenario for 2nd Group of Users:

This group users shall be able to edit only one field “Inventory Number” under the tab “General”, rest all fields should be in read-only mode.

Following user to be assigned to 2nd group:

FI_TEST_2

Solution:

  1. Go to t-code: SHD0
  2. Provide Transaction Code AS02

  1. Specify name for your Transaction Variant

  1. Click on Create button. Select any existing asset number with company code.

Hit the Enter key. This will lead to following pop-up window.

Here input Screen variant short text, as highlighted:

Click on continue button to close pop-up window.

Now input the required information (Asset description, Serial number.…) in the “General” Tab.

Hit the Enter key and another pop-up window will appear:

Here system shows all standard Asset Master data tab names. Under column “Invisible” you can activate checkbox of any single or multiple tabs if you need to hide these for specific user, group of users or all users.

In this scenario, I am going to hide all fields except the “Personnel Number” field so I will skip this pop-up window without making any changes.

Important note: If your requirement is to show only Time-dependent tab and hide all other tabs, then you must keep visible its preceding tab, that is, the “General” tab.

Similarly, to show only “Assignment” tab, you must keep visible its preceding tabs “General” and “Time-dependent”. If suppose, you make invisible the tabs preceding to the target tab, then it will cause inconsistency, and system will not allow you to save any changes in AS02.

Keep clicking the continue button till you reach following pop-up screen:

As highlighted, I have activated all the checkboxes under column “Output only” because business requirement is to keep all fields read-only under the tab “General”.

  1. Keep clicking the Continue button till you reach the “Time-dependent” tab.

Here input the required data (e.g. Cost Center, Funds Center) and hit the Enter Key.

Again pop-up window will appear. Keep clicking continue button until you approach the following screen:

Here I have activated the checkbox of all fields except “Personnel number” under the column “Output only” since my objective is to allow user to edit only one field “Personnel number” and keep all other fields in display mode.

Now we have achieved our objective for 1st group of users, so we can click on  button.

In next step, system will ask to provide ABAP package and assign the Workbench Transport Request. Consult the ABAP consultant to provide the existing package or create a new one for you.

  1. Go back to main screen of Transaction Variant (SHD0).

Under tab “Standard Variant”, select the sub-tab “Variant Groups”

Here, click on create button to define your Group name.

Assign the first user in the field “User”

Click on  button and system will make assignment with confirmation message.

Now click on  button.

This will complete the user assignment.

Similarly, assign the next user the same way until all users under this group are assigned.

To confirm the assignment, click on  button.

The X mark shows all the users assigned to a certain group.

You can click on  button if you want to record the user assignment in transport request.

This completes the settings for Transaction/Screen variant and the user assignment to group.

  1. Go back to main screen of SHD0 and click on Test button (Short key F8) to test the outcome.

You can see all the fields are in display mode, except the “Personnel Number” field.

This setting is valid only to those users whose logon ID is assigned to Transaction Variant Group.

If you login with any other user ID, system will show all fields in edit mode, as normal.

This completes the testing for first scenario.

  1. Commencing with second scenario, we will make all fields display only, except the “Inventory Number” field for a new group of users.

All steps will be same as explained for the first scenario, here are key points to concentrate:

  1.                Create a new Transaction Variant
  2.                Checkbox of all fields will be activated except the “Inventory Number” field so it can be edited by assigned user.

     3.                 A new Group shall be created and different user(s) assigned to it.

4.               User assignment confirmation

5.               Final Testing with assigned user logon

This completes the settings and testing for our second scenario as well.

Conclusion: You can control not only Master Data tabs but also fields status which will vary user to user. Both Transaction Variant settings will work at same time on all assigned users in respective groups, hence authorization control is implemented on parallel basis without involving BASIS or ABAP development (except package creation).

Let me know if you have any feedback or any clarity required in this blog. I hope you have enjoyed the read.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Categories can contain either other categories or pro...
entry.content:
Category can technically have any of these combinations:

  • Category (Supercategory, Subcategory). See CategoryCategoryRelation.
  • Product. See CategoryProductRelation.
  • Category (Supercategory, Subcategory), Product.
  • No Category, No Product.

entry.title: Re: Accounting entry - WIP, Variance Calculation & Settle...
entry.content:
Dear All,

1st of all, we should understand the concept of cost by order which will make our understanding of WIP easy. In product cost by order, we can calculate either WIP or Variance as we can't calculate both. It depends on the status of the order; if the status of an order is REL, only then WIP can be calculated. Accounting entry is also generated which is :

WIP A/c(bal.sheet)Dr. and change in WIP A/c(P&L) Cr.

Note#1: as all these activities are done at the month end and if the status of an order gets changed to Delivered or TECO, then only variances can be calculated and then settled.

Variance entry:

COGM Dr/Cr.

Variance A/C Dr./Cr.

Note#2. Hence, WIP or Variance totally depends on the status of an order unlike product cost by period in which we can have both WIP and variance in product cost collector.

entry.title: Negative Quantity Withdrawn in RESB after cancel GI
entry.content:
Hello experts,

The issue is as follows:

We are performing backflushes in COR6 (movement type 261) along with Goods Receipt (101).

When some raw material needs to be canceled, users are entering COR6, changing the movement type from 261 to 262, setting the quantity and batch, and saving the transaction.

This is causing a problem where the field RESB-ENMNG (quantity withdrawn) turns into a negative value.

I believe the movement itself updates the field, but the confirmation does not.

When they try to access the material document (MIGO) or enter COR6 again, it results in a termination with the error: "DYNPRO_FIELD_CONVERSION".

Is this the correct way to cancel a Goods Issue (GI) that was made to a Process Order? Note that these materials are EWM-managed (decentralized).

Please share any insights or suggestions.

entry.title: Editable custom field in line item @ ME51N
entry.content:
Hi,

We need to display our custom Z fields(created in EBAN table using Z append structure) at line item level in ME51N/ME52N(PR creation and change).While the custom fields are getting displayed at line item level, but this could not be edited or shown at line item level as editable columns in ME51/ME52N(please find attached "PR Line Item" screen shot attached).

Did R&D in SDN community and some debug, found that below were some of our observations:

1. While debugging and from understanding from SDN, in program SAPLMEGUI(include LMEGUICJM), method BUILD_DYNPRO_FIELDS (LCL_REQ_ITEM_GRID) in MY_DYNPRO_FIELDS. If we change the metafield and make fieldcatalog(gt_fieldcatalog) EDIT for our custom fields as X, then these columns are displayed editable(please find "Debug1","Debug2","Debug3" screen shot for this).

2. Searched in SPRO settings to achieve this(as understood from SDN), SPRO--> Material Management-->Purchasing-->Purchase Requisition-->Define Screen Layout at Document Level. But here also we could not see our custom fields.

3. We could see BADI such as MEGUI_GRID_ENHANCEMENT for enabling our custom field metafield and fieldcatalogue(But here it is mentioned as "SAP Internal")

Doubt:

1. Adding editable custom fields - Is this possible via SPRO?

2.If not possible via SPRO, Is there any BADI which supports this case

3.If not possible, is it advisable to create any implicit enhancement in MEGUI_GRID_ENHANCEMENT implementation or so

With Regards,
Jagan

entry.title: Re: Planned depreciation not distributed in each month
entry.content: Hello, I assume you have managed to solve that problem quite long ago already, but just in case it may be helpful for others running into the same issue: the consideration would be to check whether expired useful life in years and periods at start of the fiscal year calculated automatically based on the useful life is not overwritten or, if so, whether it is correct. For example, if this is a legacy asset to be transferred in the June 2024, what should specified here are the years and periods expired up to the start of 2024, and not the transfer period - June - itself; otherwise, it is assumed that the whole Useful life is complete and for that particular asset all remaining depreciation is posted in one period. Again, that would only make sense if there are just several assets with such an issue, otherwise, it might be of course a matter of configuration for the calculation methods in defined SAP.

entry.title: Re: AI in 2025 - predictions or guessing game
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm not a fan of Rabbit AI, but I have to admit that I was impressed by its concept and design. However, in an era where we carry smartphones in our pockets all the time, this additional device in such a shape is completely impractical. The glasses form would be more useful, but I also have some doubts about whether it will catch on.

However, agents themselves are only as powerful as the tools we provide them. Artificial intelligence can truly shine when we equip it with the right tooling. This is both fascinating and terrifying.

SAP doesn't seem to have fully chosen a single path for now. On one hand, it seems that opening up to the world and providing such a framework would be beneficial for users. However, let's not forget that the company is meant to make a profit, and that is its main goal. And SAP is great at making money...

BR

entry.title: Re: I created the SAP GUI AI Agent
entry.content:
Hi @LeandroRibeiro 

SAP GUI is, on one hand, a very specific interface, but on the other hand, it's quite typical. If you think about it more closely, you'll find that it shares many common elements with web pages. You have a transaction name that serves a similar role to a URL for web pages. You have elements like buttons and text fields. Additionally, there are, of course, menus and various other elements like dropdown menus within the page content. So it's natural that the tools are somewhat similar to those used for managing websites with artificial intelligence.

The entire challenge of creating an agent lies in preparing a type of "web scraping" that works with the SAP GUI. In the case of the improved version, I was able to extract more information using just text. However, in the first video, you can see that screenshots played a bigger role. For now, screenshots are used where I don't yet support native elements.

As I mentioned in the first post, the whole thing is based on a LangGraph and uses SAP scripting both for handling the interface and for reading the content of the page. The rest is done by the LLM.

BR

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2502
entry.content:
Overview

Providing a significant number of new features is an important goal of release upgrades to deliver innovations to our customers and partners. There are four major release upgrades per year following a predefined schedule as described in this blog post.

An upgrade is intended as a “no event” for customers and partners. For example, only compatible changes of released objects (APIs) will be delivered. We recommend performing a test of the custom-built productive apps in a test environment for the new release. The complexity of the technical dependencies between the custom-built apps and the underlying environment cannot be tested isolated, only including the ABAP environment on SAP-side. Therefore, we offer you to pre-upgrade selected systems 22 days* in advance to test the applications prior to the planned standard-upgrade.

* For 2502 upgrade, the pre-upgrade period will be enhanced to 3 weeks (from 2 weeks since 2108 upgrade to 3 weeks). Starting with 2505 upgrade, a pre-upgrade period of 4 weeks is planned.   

 

Purpose

This option enables a customer or partner to test the applications they have already developed on top of the new release a couple of days before the standard-upgrade. With SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk"), we follow the approach of keeping released objects stable and provide all enhancements in a compatible fashion. Early testing is an additional measure to ensure proper software quality and might be used to detect unforeseen regressions. However, this option is not intended to test new features or to consume new features earlier.

 

Approach

The standard-upgrade of all systems to release 2502 is planned for February 15/16.

A customer or partner can nominate one or more already existing systems for the pre-upgrade. A nominated system will be upgraded on January 24 within a planned downtime from 8:00 to 16:00 CET.

We recommend nominating a non-development system, such as a test system. The nomination of a development system is technically also possible, but fixes or other changes must not be imported into another system with a lower release. The nomination of a productive system doesn’t make sense because the purpose of the pre-upgrade is the re-test of the existing application before the productive system gets the new release.

According to the approach explained above, this offer is only relevant for customers or partners having at least two systems.

There might be situations where a correction of custom objects needs to be deployed. Such corrections are possible if the development / correction system is on the older release and is imported into the pre-upgraded system with the higher release.

Except potential fixes, the pre-upgrade is based on the same version as the standard-upgrade.

 

Costs

This offering is optional and free of any additional charges.

 

Planned New Features

The planned new features will be published on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment on January 17. This list gives you an overview of potential areas for re-testing. The purpose of the pre-upgrade is not to consume these features before the standard-upgrade, because the documentation on help.sap.com will be delivered later before the standard-upgrade. It would also not be possible to transport code of such an adoption, because subsequent systems are not yet on the new release during the pre-upgrade phase.

Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase.

 

Time Schedule

The following figure summarizes the general approach:

Milestones for release 2502:

  • December 18: Nomination of system(s) is possible in the Landscape Portal
  • January 18: Announcement of the planned new features
  • January 21: Pre-upgrade systems are known
  • January 24: Pre-upgrade
  • February 13: Potential decision to postpone the upgrade
  • February 15/16: Standard-upgrade

 

System Nomination

To nominate a system, please register the system(s) by January 18 (EOB) latest by using the Landscape Portal. You can find further information about Accessing the Landscape Portal and about Register Systems for Pre-Upgrade. Please do not create customer tickets for system nomination like in previous pre-upgrades.

With such a nomination, the customer agrees to an extraordinary planned maintenance on January 24 (see above).

 

Report an Issue

Issues should be reported as part of the regression test phase by January 31 (EOB) to allow for a sufficient time frame for issue analysis and potential corrections on SAP-side.

Please create a customer ticket by using component BC-CP-ABA.

If a detected issue is likely to endanger the productive use after the standard-upgrade, you should use ticket priority very high despite the fact that the pre-upgrade system is not a productive system.

 

Exceptional Process in Case of Unresolved Priority Very High Issues

In case of the aligned decision on Wednesday, February 12 (based on unresolved priority very high tickets) to postpone the upgrade, a dedicated mitigation process needs to be started together with the respective customer or partner, with the following boundary conditions:

  • Resolving the open priority very high issues remains the highest priority for both, SAP and the customer or partner, as it is also the prerequisite for the upgrade.
  • According to the estimated issue resolution, catching up the upgrade needs to be planned.
  • The upgrade leads to a business downtime of 6 – 8 hours.
  • The upgrade must be scheduled within 7 calendar days the latest after fixing all remaining open priority very high issues.
  • We will not provide any fixes for the old release after the original date of the standard-upgrade to the new release.

In case business-critical issues are detected after the upgrade:

  • Issues need to be reported as priority very high issues and will be processed accordingly.
  • Going back to the release before upgrade is not an option. Therefore, resolving the issues in the new release with the appropriate priority is the required approach.

 

Summary

We recommend performing regression tests in pre-upgraded test systems to avoid day-1 impacts of productive custom apps after the standard-upgrade.

entry.title: Re: Upcoming Deprecation of VBScript, its Impact on SAP GUI Scripting and Resulting Strategy
entry.content:
Hello Mattia @bergom,

I think your approach is very good and you can take it easy, but the horizon is getting closer. With the upcoming release of Windows 11 24H2 is VBScript available as a feature on demand (FOD) enabled by default. It is planned in two to three years as FOD but disabled by default and much later to remove it.

Just as VBScript is available to you today, you can also use JScript. Call

wscript yourJavaScriptFile.js

in your console.

JScript offers a COM interface like VBScript, so it is very easy to use SAP GUI Scripting with JScript. Here an example code.

function main() {

  try {

    var SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI");
    if (
      !SapGuiAuto || SapGuiAuto === "null" || SapGuiAuto === "undefined"
    ) {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get object SAPGUI");
      return;
    }

    var app = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine;
    if (!app || app === "null" || app === "undefined") {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get scripting engine");
      return;
    }

    app.HistoryEnabled = false;

    var connection = app.Children(0);
    if (
      !connection || connection === "null" || connection === "undefined"
    ) {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get connection");
      return;
    }

    if (connection.DisabledByServer === true) {
      WScript.Echo("Connection disabled by server");
      return;
    }

    var session = connection.Children(0);
    if (!session || session === "null" || session === "undefined") {
      WScript.Echo("Can't get session");
      return;
    }

    if (session.Busy === true) {
      WScript.Echo("Session busy");
      return;
    }

    if (session.Info.IsLowSpeedConnection === true) {
      WScript.Echo("Session has low speed connection");
      return;
    }

    session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nse16";
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0);
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtDATABROWSE-TABLENAME").text = "TADIR";
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtDATABROWSE-TABLENAME").caretPosition = 5;
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0);
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(31);
    session.findById("wnd[1]/tbar[0]/btn[0]").press();
    session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(3);
    session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[3]").press();

    app.HistoryEnabled = true;

  } catch (exception) {
    if (!exception.description) {
      WScript.Echo("An exception " + (exception.number & 0xFFFF) +
        " occurred");
    } else {
      WScript.Echo("An exception " + (exception.number & 0xFFFF) +
        " occurred:\n" + exception.description);
    }
  }

}

main();

Sure, the syntax differs between VBScript and JScript but the basic approach remains the same. In my opinion, learning a new programming language is not that difficult if you have already mastered a similar one. Approach things calmly, do not let it drive you crazy and you will be successful after a short time. You can do this now, regardless whether the backend uses SAP HANA or not.

I have no doubt that you will make it.

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Overall timeout/deadline for CPI iflow
entry.content:
Hi @juankinspain 

Unfortunately, there is no functionality out of the box to achieve what you seek. As you described, any solution will need to be a combination of scripts so you can achieve it with code and loops until you find the correct condition.

I want to share some guidelines because I have been in a similar situation where iFlows have been running for some time (more than an hour), and in those cases, I reached out to product support (support.sap.com) because it was not working as I expected.

  1. It is not recommended to have iFlows, which require hours to complete, especially if the iFlows are not restartable (that means if it fails, it can be restarted from the point of failure). Ideally, the logic should be split into multiple iFlows, which, all together, produce the required outcome. Ideally, these sub-flows should be designed to be restartable, so even if some flow fails, it gets eventually restarted and completed. The more time an iFlow takes, the bigger the probability that it fails. Reasons:
  1. iFlow runs out of memory, and the iFlow gets killed
  2. Any other iFlow on the node produces an out-of-memory situation, and all iFlows running at the same node get killed
  3. During software updates or security patches, an iFlow takes 5 minutes to complete; afterward, it gets killed

I hope this helps you.
Best regards 🖖🏻
Ricardo

entry.title: Re: Make custom field clickable and redirect to another app
entry.content:
Hello JustinC1,

It is possible to add a custom field at the Journal Entry Item level with the field type defined as a "Web Address." This custom field can then be populated using a BAdI during the creation or update of a journal entry. However, the field be configured to be clickable, but it is not possible to assign a specific label to this field, it will look like complete URL.

KR,

Aliaksandra

entry.title: BAS Studio Setup, Cloud Connector Integration and Application Deployment Steps to CF and on-premise
entry.content:
SAP BTP Trial Account, BAS Studio Setup, Cloud Connector Integration, and Application Deployment Steps

The Importance of SAP BTP and Integration: A Robust Foundation for Digital Transformation

In the era of digital transformation, having a flexible and reliable IT infrastructure is essential for businesses to enhance efficiency and maintain a competitive edge. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is a modern cloud platform designed to meet these demands, enabling businesses to accelerate innovation, optimize processes, and develop new digital solutions.

A key component of this platform is SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), which provides developers with a powerful and user-friendly development environment. With BAS, creating, developing, and deploying cloud-based applications becomes faster and more efficient.

By integrating tools like SAP Cloud Connector, businesses can seamlessly connect on-premise systems with SAP BTP, ensuring secure and compliant data exchange. This integration supports hybrid system architectures, allowing companies to leverage their existing ABAP systems while benefiting from the flexibility and scalability of the cloud.

This comprehensive guide outlines the step-by-step process for setting up SAP BTP, configuring BAS, integrating with on-premise systems, and deploying applications to both Cloud Foundry and ABAP environments. Whether you're exploring SAP BTP for the first time or aiming to streamline your development processes, this document serves as a valuable resource for navigating the complexities of modern enterprise technology.

Creating Subaccount in a BTP Trial Account and Adding Services

  1. Visit https://account.hana.ondemand.com/ to create a BTP trial account.
  2. After registering, navigate to the Trial Home page and proceed to the Trial Account section to create a subaccount.
  3. Access the subaccount you created and load the required contents via the Entitlements section.
    • First, click the Edit button.
    • Then, click the Add Service Plans button to add the necessary services.

       



Business Application Studio Setup and Required Content Installation

To set up Business Application Studio (BAS) and install the necessary content, you can follow either of these two approaches:

  1. Adding the Service to an Existing Subaccount
    If you have already created a subaccount, you can add Business Application Studio to it by selecting the service addition option. Alternatively, you can directly go to the BOOSTER menu, search for "Business App Studio," or find it in the list. From there, you can create the subaccount while setting up BAS.
  2. Setting Up the Services
    After the installation is complete, go to the Entitlements menu, click Configure Entitlements, and add the following services in the order listed:
    • SAP Build Work Zone
    • SAP Business Application Studio
    • SAP HANA Cloud
    • SAP HANA Schemas & HDI Containers
    • Mobile Services
    • Cloud Foundry Runtime/Environment
  3. Creating the Business Application Studio Instance
    Next, create the BAS instance from the Instance menu.
  4. Running Business Application Studio
    Once BAS is installed and the services are configured, start the Business Application Studio.
  5. Creating a New Workspace
    After running BAS, create a new workspace for your project.
  6. Ready to Use
    Your Business Application Studio is now ready to use.

     

This setup process ensures that all required services are added and that your BAS environment is configured and running, ready for your development work.

 

Installing and Configuring SAP Cloud Connector

After setting up Business Application Studio (BAS), if you wish to integrate it with an ABAP system that uses a VPN, you will need to create a connection point using SAP Cloud Connector. SAP Cloud Connector provides the following functionalities:

  • Secure connection between SAP BTP and on-premise systems.
  • Controls data flow between systems.
  • Ensures compliance with security policies.

You can download the SAP Cloud Connector installation file from the following link: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud

Installation on Windows:

  1. Double-click the installation file to start the process.
  2. Accept the license agreement.
  3. Choose the installation directory (e.g., C:\SAP\CloudConnector).
  4. Click Install and wait for the installation to complete.

Step 1: Starting the Cloud Connector

  1. Navigate to Start > SAP Cloud Connector to launch the application.
  2. It will automatically open in your default browser. Alternatively, you can manually open the URL: http://localhost:8443 .

Step 2: Logging In

  • Username: Administrator
  • Password: manage

If you have previously logged in and changed your password but forgot it, you can reset the password by editing the users.xml file located in the C:\SAP\scc20\config directory. Change the password field to the encrypted value: "3F282FCBA8933E03A65A6DC92A27DE8396961E2F". This will reset the password to manage. After saving the file, restart SAP Cloud Connector from the Services menu.

Step 3: Configuring the SAP BTP Connection

  1. To establish a connection between the subaccount you created in SAP BTP and Cloud Connector, go to the Cloud Connector Administration panel and click Add Subaccount.

     

  2. Skip the page requesting host, port, user, and password. Enter the subaccount details from your SAP BTP account.


  3. Navigate to the Cloud To On-Premise tab.
  4. Click the Add System Mapping button and fill in the following information:
    • Backend Type: ABAP System
    • Protocol: HTTPS or HTTP
    • Host: e.g., 10.10.10.10
    • Port: e.g., 8000
  5. Click Save and test the connection.

Mapping Configuration:

  • When creating the system mapping, if you enter '/' in the URL path field, all subdirectories will be included automatically, eliminating the need to specify each subdirectory individually.

With these steps completed, SAP Cloud Connector will be configured and ready for use in integrating your ABAP system with SAP BTP.

 

Establishing a Connection Between BAS Workspace and ABAP System

After creating the connection point for your on-premise system (e.g., TST system) using Cloud Connector, you can establish a connection between your Business Application Studio (BAS) workspace and the system in two ways:

Method 1: Direct Connection from Business Application Studio

  1. In BAS, go to the Service section from the left-hand menu.
  2. Select Add New Service under your account and enter the required details.
  3. After entering the details, click on the service. If a green button appears in the corner, it means the information was entered correctly and the connection is established successfully.

Method 2: Setting Up the Destination in BTP Account

  1. Log in to your BTP account and select your subaccount.
  2. In the left-hand menu, select Connectivity, then choose Destinations.
  3. Before proceeding, click on Cloud Connectors and verify if the access point you created in the Cloud Connector application appears under Exposed Back-End Systems as "available."
  4. Now, from the Destinations section, click Create Destination to create a destination for the service.
  5. Fill in the following details:
    • https://adress:port
    • SAP Client, User, and Password: Provide the credentials for your ABAP backend system.
    • Web IDE System: This should match the information for your ABAP backend system.
    • URL: Enter the URL in the format:
    • Proxy Type: Set to OnPremise.
    • Authentication: Set to BasicAuthentication.
  6. In the Web IDE Usage field, add the following values:
    • odata_abap: For Gateway operations.
    • ui5_execute_abap: To execute UI5 applications from the ABAP repository.
    • dev_abap: For development and deployment operations.
      If additional functionalities are required, you can search for other Web IDE usages by querying WebIDEUsage.
  7. After entering the details, click Check to verify the connection.

     

Once the connection is successfully verified, your BAS workspace will be connected to the ABAP system.

 

Deploying the Application to On-Premise System or Cloud Foundry

After completing the project, open the project directory in the terminal and run the following commands:

  1. npm install
  2. npm build

This will create the node_modules and dist folders.

Then, run the following command:

  • npm run deploy-config
    • or alternatively:
    • npx fiori add deploy-config

At this point, we have two options for deployment: Cloud Foundry or ABAP system.

Option 1: Deploying to Cloud Foundry

  1. When deploying to Cloud Foundry, select CF and it will prompt you for your Cloud Foundry account credentials.
  2. Enter your BTP account email and password. Once entered, it will establish the connection to Cloud Foundry, and if successful, you will be able to deploy the application.
  3. Before proceeding, ensure that the Cloud Foundry service is added and running in your BTP account. When you enter the Cloud Foundry section in your BTP Subaccount, you should see your space with deployed applications.
  4. Additionally, if your application is published as an HTML5 app, it will also appear in the HTML5 Applications section in the left-hand menu of your BTP Subaccount.

Option 2: Deploying to ABAP System

Before starting, create a Transport Request number in your ABAP system.

  1. In BAS, select the ABAP system in the terminal section.
  2. You will then be prompted to choose the destination (the service-destination you added earlier).
  3. Next, you will be asked for the SAPUI5 ABAP Repository. Enter the name of the BSP Application you created under the package.
  4. Afterward, you will be asked for the Deployment Description. Enter your description.
  5. Then, you will be prompted for the Package. Select Enter Manually and provide the name of the package you created.
  6. Finally, input the Transport Request number and complete the process.

Even if the deploy-config is created for the ABAP system, BAS may ask for additional information, and the generated files may not be sufficient to complete the deployment. Therefore, follow these steps:

  1. Open the ui5-deploy.yaml file in your project.
  2. Under the configuration: section, add the following:

credentials:

  username: env:USERNAME

  password: env:PASSWORD

  1. Under the target: section, add:

client: env:CLIENTNUMBER

auth: basic

  1. Save the file.

After making these changes, run npm run deploy in the terminal, and your project will be deployed to the ABAP system with the necessary configurations.

Check SE80 to verify that the project has been successfully deployed.

Lastly, in the SICF menu, enter the name of your BSP application and confirm that the application service is running correctly.

entry.title: Re: Query List of Otbound Deliveries from SAP S/4 Hana Pu...
entry.content:
Hi @Hanumath 
I will assume you have configured the corresponding communication scenario.
The URI is the following:

 

https://myxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV;v=0002/A_OutbDeliveryHeader?$top=3&$filter=CreationDate ge datetime'2016-08-16T00:00:00'&$inlinecount=allpages

 

I recommend using the encoded format

 

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV;v=0002/A_OutbDeliveryHeader?$top=3&$filter=CreationDate%20ge%20datetime'2024-12-16T00%3A00%3A00'&$inlinecount=allpages

 

I hope this helps you.

Best regards 🖖🏻
Ricardo

entry.title: Rental condition type should auto calculate the unit price in the manage contract app.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement in RE FX lease accounting, where the rental condition type should auto calculate the unit price in the manage contract app.

Current standard process:

When we insert the condition type in the “Manage Contract” app, rental condition will be added, and unit price is updated manually in the section as per below screen shot.

 

Selecting the relevant condition from the list:

When we add the condition, the unit price will show as 0 as per the standard functionality.

We enter the condition amount manually in the unit price section.

 

As per our requirement for the automate calculation based on the condition type and company code combination: The Unit Price needs to be calculated automatically based on a formula...

X represents Turbine rent

A represents No. of Wind Turbines (value of 6)

B represents MW capacity of Wind Turbines (value of 20,0000)

X will be calculated as follows (A x B = X)

6 x 20,000 = 120,000

So, X (Turbine rent) will have an automatic calculated value of 120,000.

Please let us know, what is the best way to achieve this requirement. 

your prompt response would be highly appreciated 😊

Thanks,

Karthik

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

SAP Real Estate Management

entry.title: Seeking a dependency for SAP API Proxy bundle generation similar to openapi2apigee.
Re: SUM SP19: Password for 'sapsso' does not work, please reenter
entry.content:
Execute the following command as user <sid>adm from the command prompt:


rsecssfx list


Check the listed output for the following entries:
DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_USER
DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_PASSWORD

 

Execute below commands with sidadm:

rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_USER sapsso -plain
rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SSODB_PASSWORD <password>

rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SADB_USER sapsa -plain

rsecssfx put DB_CONNECT/SYB/SADB_PASSWORD <Password>

entry.title: Achieving Sustainability With SAP Asset Performance Management By Leveraging Industrial Systems.
entry.content:
This blog focuses on the process of setting up Industrial Systems in SAP APM (Asset Performance Management). As part of SAP APM 2411 release, SAP introduced a new application named Industrial Systems to structure and track individual system elements within larger system setups.

The Industrial System is a master data object in SAP Asset Performance Management and is based on industry standard IEC/ISO-81346 for systems that describe several aspects of a technical system. An Industrial System is a set of inter-related objects considered in a defined context as a whole and enables functional, product-related, and location-related views of a technical system.

What is IEC/ISO 81346 Standard:

IEC 81346-1 establishes general principles for the structuring of systems including structuring of information about systems. It also provides rules and guidance for the formulation of unambiguous reference designations for objects in any systems. 

IEC 81346-2 establishes classification schemes with defined object classes and their associated letter codes, and primarily intended for use in reference designations. Designators give an unambiguous way of identifying the aspect element in the system.

Fact Sheet:

IEC International Standards provide the technical foundation that allows countries to increase national innovation and economic productivity as well as upgrade and retrofit cleaner and environmentally sound technologies and industrial processes.

The IEC 81346 standards contribute to Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), including  SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth), 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) & 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities).

    

In essence,  these standards foster innovation and promote sustainable industrialization by building resilient infrastructure.

Before we dive in to understand industrial systems in SAP Asset Performance Management, we must first understand LEGO bricks. So, here we go.

LEGO Technic NEOM McLaren Formula E Race Car 42169

Building the LEGO Technic NEOM McLaren Formula E Race Car 42169System within the System...

The crux of the Industrial Systems is, the component systems create technical systems and those technical systems create main systems.

Let us dive in...

First things first, enable the Industrial Systems Configuration toggle for Industrial Systems in the Configuration Manager Application and created a reference designator library.

Industry Domain describes the area in which a particular industrial system operates. For instance, I have created MFG - Manufacturing & PRO - Process Industry in addition to the pre-shipped industry domains. Industry domains are a mandatory part of reference designator libraries, which are structured according to IEC/ISO 81346.

Reference Designator Source is the classification standard for the reference designator. 

Aspect is the way of viewing a system in a specified way. A system can contain more than one aspect, with each aspect describing different views of the same system.

We can create any aspect, but it must be assigned to one of the pre-shipped standard aspect types.

A relationship type is defined as the type of relationship between system elements. Relationship type holds the metadata for a relationship, such as source and target entity types, applicable aspect types (Function, Product, Location).

Note: Creation of new relationship types is not supported with the current version of Industrial Systems.

After completing the "Configuration Manager" set up, let us proceed to create "Reference Designator Libraries".

Reference designators are identifiers based on an industry standard library. A Reference Designator Library in SAP Asset Performance Management contains multiple Reference Designator Object Classes.

Note:

  • Schema Category "Aspect Oriented" can only be applied to system elements  of a selected "Aspect Type".
  • Schema Category "Object Oriented" can be applied to all system elements  independent of the "Aspect Type". -  For instance, I have chosen "Object Oriented".

Once the data is updated, we can change the status to "Released" for further processing. The status "Reopened" can be used to edit after the released status.

Now, Let us create the "Industrial System".

Here is my business use case:

I have chosen the "Cooling Water Circulation system" , which is crucial in the Calcination Process Industry. To define hierarchical structure for the "Cooling Tower System" in my calcination plant as per IEC 81346, I have classified the system based on Function, Product and Location aspects.

  • Functional Aspect: Heat Dissipation, Water Circulation, Drive Function and Control & Regulation.
  • Product Aspect: Heat Dissipation System, Pump System1, Pump System2 & Pump System3.
  • Location Aspect: Power Control Center, Motor Control Center, EX-01 Panel , EX-02 Panel & EX-03 Panel, Cooling Tower, Pump House-1.

Also, for instance, I have considered the industrial system perspective "Plant" to represent cooling water circulation system, i.e. Cooling Tower.

  • Equipment: If we want to assign or link an equipment from SAP ERP system to Industrial System.
  • Functional Location: If we want to assign or link an equipment from SAP ERP system to Industrial System.
  • Plant: If we want to create an asset structure for the production plant.

The prefix signs used to indicate the type of "Aspect" in a reference designator code, i.e.

  • = When relating to the function aspect of an object.
  • - When relating to the product aspect of the object.
  • + When relating to the location aspect of the object.

Function Aspect:

Product Aspect:

Location Aspect:

Adding a Product Individual to a Product Aspect. Product individual represents a technical object (equipment or functional location) present in SAP ERP, which instantiates a system element of product aspect type.

Adding a relationship between the system elements and another node.

Mini Note: The connecting arrows only point in the direction of the primary relationships, i.e. Child Of, Realized By, Located at, and Instantiated By.

We can view the basic details of the system element.

This brings me to the end of my process walk-through. I hope my blog proves useful to reliability engineering team, and I look forward seeing them use SAP APM Industrial System application to foster sustainable maintenance operations.

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy. Chelikam


 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

My LinkedIn Profile: www.linkedin.com/in/pardhreddyc

entry.title: Costo Estándar SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Buen día Colegas, gusto en saludarles en esta época. 

En un ambiente de prueba en SAP S/4 Hana, estoy analizado una programación de costo estándar para un material producto terminado a través de la CK40N, en el análisis observo que el kg por ejemplo se programa a $1.40/kg (validado en MM03) y en la etapa de análisis de la CK40N, refleja que el costo a programar será de $1.36/Kg. ¿Alguien sabe si programando el costo final del producto terminado con la CK40N algún paso se está saltando para que resulte esa diferencia $1.40 vs $1.36? No sé si será por los costos de la receta del sku terminado.

Agradezco los comentarios al respecto. Feliz fin de año 2024 y mis mejores deseos para este año nuevo 2025.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM: sequencing picking WTs for multiple Stock Ty...
entry.content:
Hi,

"Is there a way, within standard configuration, to ensure that all picking tasks are sequenced according to the pick path, regardless of stock type?"

The sorting of the warehouse tasks is defined by the profile you defined in the node "Define Sort Rules for Warehouse Tasks" and assigned to the warehouse order creation rule in the field "WO Sorting". So what have you got there? But it might get messed up if you have assigned a different sort rule in the "Packaging Profile" (never tested that).

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Re: Vendor consignment stock vs Customer consignment stoc...
entry.content: i think the definition and consignment terms and conditions are a little confusing for you. Consignment is a relationship/partnership between a vendor and its customer. Since the stock is placed at the customer's location, there is little to no way for the supplier to know when the inventory provided has been consumed; the customer settles the invoice using MRKO. if for instance, a change were to occur in prices and conditions, both parties can work together and update it in info records.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD - WHAT IS IT ABOUT?
entry.content:
@RahulBhatia20 wrote:

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Explained in Detail

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is a robust, cloud-based enterprise resource planning (ERP) solution designed to enhance and streamline business processes across various industries. 

I am currently working as a SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Architect in the Public Sector industry and responsible for the Architecture, Design, and implementation for a large Public sector organization and can see tremendous advantages in why the Public sector would benefit from the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud edition. In my current project, we are implementing SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud 2408 utilizing the Central Business Configuration (CBC) tool to activate the specified industry relevant business processes.  Some of the Core Features offered by SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud solution that we are seeing in the project are as follows:

1. It comes with a Pre-Configured System: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud has pre-defined business processes that we can activate and implement. This allows organizations to adapt to the standard processes easily and without requiring any enhancements. With SAP's Central Business in place to support this, organizations can have their business processes configured from one single tool in the cloud. Central Business Configuration for their implementation. As Cloud is becoming much advanced with the use of AI & ML capabilities, public sector organizations such as UK Councils are seeing the benefits in the product and have started their journey by implementation of the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud solution to transform their existing landscape. 

2. Endless Scalability and Flexibility to activate new processes : Organizations can now activate business processes or use new add on features much easily than before. The new solution provides in depth insights related to how the process works with the help of Signavio and allows businesses to be in control of their system like never before. 

3.  Innovation : SAP keeps pushing regular updates and also keep advancing the functionalities that it offers with the latest use of AI & ML. Fiori apps are like never before, as it integrates with AI capabilities providing better view of the reporting and also enabling business users to easily learn how AI is redefining the business processes. 

4. Faster Integration :  SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud integrates much faster and effectively with other systems whether it be an existing SAP system or any other ERP or Non ERP system. SAP provides built in and readily available API's that helps organizations transition their data exchange smoothly. 

5. Impact on Environment and how AI is helping achieve Sustainability : SAP's S/4 HANA Public cloud offeres various new AI enabled features that now allows organizations to keep track of their carbon emissions and how their business processes are affecting the environment in todays fasted paced world.  With new reporting capabilities such as SAP Analytics Cloud, business can meet their sustainability goals much effectively and helps them to make better decisions in the longer run. 

As the product is getting matured further with AI and ML capabilities, it is fair to say that SAP's S/4 HANA Public Cloud has enormous benefits to offer to the Public Sector. 

I will continue to share insights from my existing project and how we can leverage the key benefits from SAP's latest offerings and AI enabled capabilities. 

Hello @RahulBhatia20 nysdmv ,
Your insights into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, particularly for the Public Sector, are invaluable! Highlighting its pre-configured systems, seamless scalability, and rapid integration truly showcases its potential to revolutionize public sector operations. Plus, SAP's focus on AI, ML, and sustainability aligns perfectly with modern organizational goals.

Are others in similar roles also seeing significant impacts from these new features? 

Any additional specifics you'd like to share about your project? 


Best Regards,
Thomas Brown

entry.title: How to Change Date Formats in Custom CDS Views in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Statement:
In the Custom CDS View app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there are scenarios where a report requires a column with date values to be displayed in a specific format based on business needs. However, the app does not provide a direct function to change the date format. To address this, you can leverage a calculated column and utilize predefined functions such as CONCAT and SUBSTRING. By using these functions, you can manipulate the date values into the desired format.
I will demonstrate how to achieve this using an example to address such requirements effectively.

Solution:

Scenario:
Suppose there is a column in a standard CDS view containing date values in the MM/DD/YYYY format (Figure 1.1), but you need the date to be displayed in the DD-MM-YYYY format in your report.


Fig 1.1

Solution:
Step 1: Create a calculative column in a custom CDS.




Step 2: Copy the below code and paste in the code space and click on Apply.

concat( substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7 , 2 ) , concat( '-' , concat( substring(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5 , 2 ),concat( '-' , substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1 , 4 ) ) )))



Explanation of code :

The SUBSTRING function used to extracts specific parts of the string based on the start position and length.
The CONCAT function is used to combine two or more strings into a single string.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7, 2) extracts the day (characters 7 and 2 as 7 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5, 2) extracts the month (characters 5 and 6) as 5 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1, 4) extracts the year (characters 1 through 4) as 1 is start position and 4 is the length.

The CONCAT function combines these extracted parts and inserts the '-' character as a separator between them.

Step 3: Click on Check, and once the check is complete, click on Data Browser.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Material is having stock only at Batch level in MMBE View
entry.content: Hence, If you choose plant level, the batch number is unique in conjunction with the respective material and the plant. If you choose the material level, the batch number is unique together with the material. At the client level, the batch number is unique in the whole client
